mode_selection_rationale: Operator-directive pre-flight returned PENDING DIRECTIVE: DIRECTIVE-20260511-001-decider-throughput-audit-and-tap-out-investigation (priority=high, target_mode=blank). Per tinker.md L52-63, this overrides Mode 1-4 selection. Routed to PROP authorship mode (Mode 4-equivalent) since the directive's expected_output is a PROP-NNN file under monitor/tinker/proposals/.

modes_checked.mode_4_proposals_and_fixes: Effectively this mode — directive asked for a PROP.

directive_processed.title: Decider per-run throughput audit: is decider getting enough 'umpf'?
directive_processed.throughput_bug_verdict: REAL — confirmed by empirical evidence (67% of M1 closures are 10-14d items BAU should have caught; 19/48 current status='open' items are >=24h old; the 'pick 10 WINs' cap in decider-curmudgeon.md L421-426 is the documented proximate cause).

findings.summary: Decider's 'pick 10 WINs' Mode 1/2 cap (decider-curmudgeon.md L421-426) is causing minor/moderate curmudgeon-raised items to sit in status='open' for 5-14 days waiting for M1 to fire.
evidence: decider-curmudgeon.md L421: 'Mode 1 — Severity triage: ... pick 10 WINs with highest-severity patchable issues.'
decider-curmudgeon.md L426: 'Mode 2 — WIN cleanup: Pick 10 WINs with fewest remaining issues (1-2 each).'
Of 39 M1 closures (real, non-dryrun) with parseable ages: 67% are 10-14d items (clearly stale, BAU-missable); 10% are 7-9d (just-over-threshold); 23% are >=15d (legitimately stale-edge-case)

Current status='open' bucket: 48 items; 19 (40%) are >=24h old; the oldest is ISS-1829 at 7.34d (status=open, severity=minor, source=curmudgeon WIN-014.c4) — independently confirms operator's claim that items 'wait forever'

Operator HNOTE precedent (HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-MODE-001 2026-04-25, HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-M1-ORPHAN-QUEUE-001 2026-05-10) shows the operator has had to manually intervene twice in 17 days to force decider to process work it wasn't prioritizing — same failure pattern, recurring
findings.recommended_action: Land PROP-031 (authored this run). Specifically: add Priority 3b 'Open Bucket BAU Triage' (every status='open' item >=12h old gets triaged each run via the routing-matrix 5-action tree); remove the 'pick 10 WINs' cap in decider-curmudgeon.md Mode 1/2; add anti-tap-out clause to decider.md Critical Rules with self-test enforcement gate.

findings.summary: ISS-1829 (status='open', age=7.34d at run time) was ELIGIBLE for M1 sweep at 2026-05-11T17:24Z but was NOT swept. K_cap=30 was not reached (only 9 candidates). Possibly a found_at vs last_modified ordering bug, or the run captured a stale clone state.
evidence: open-issues.json: ISS-1829 has found_at=2026-05-04T09:20:24Z, last_modified=None, status='open', severity='minor'
M1 sweep at run 2026-05-11T17:24Z swept 9 items (ISS-1807, 1808, 1809, 1810, 1811, 1814, 1818, 1820, 1821) — all severity=minor or moderate, all source=curmudgeon

ISS-1829 is older (>7d) than several of the swept items but was not in the candidate list — likely the M1 bash helper's age threshold computation used a slightly different reference time or last_modified path that filtered ISS-1829 out by ~1 hour
findings.recommended_action: Under PROP-031, this becomes moot — Priority 3b would catch ISS-1829 (and similar 6-7d boundary items) long before M1's age threshold. Filing for awareness; no separate fix needed if PROP-031 lands.

findings.summary: Decider's latest-decider-summary.txt narrates patches applied + EXP integrations + M1 sweep counts — implicitly rewards visible-volume work over invisible-volume work like BAU triage of aged items.
evidence: latest-decider-summary.txt 2026-05-11T17:24Z: 'PATCHES (9 applied, 0 failed): WIN-001, WIN-038, WIN-010, SEC-5.0, WIN-028 [5 fixes]; PROPOSALS (8); CURMUDGEON: WIN-028.c7 popped + 4 new holes all patched; M1 SWEEP: 9 candidates...' — celebratory framing of patch volume, no surfacing of 'X status=open items not yet triaged'
Triaging a minor citation issue with wontfix-no-longer-real (a legitimate BAU outcome under PROP-031) produces no visible summary line — looks identical to no-op
LLM-behavior effect even with Critical Rules saying otherwise
findings.recommended_action: Out of scope for PROP-031 (which addresses mechanism, not framing). Flag for future follow-up: add a `bau_triage_summary` line to latest-decider-summary.txt template (e.g., '34 status=open items triaged: 5 patched, 12 wontfixed, 14 routed-to-analyst, 3 escalated'). Operator can file as a sibling directive if desired.

previous_followup.note: Did not walk PROP verification this run because the directive-override took precedence. The dispatcher rule (tinker.md L54: 'directives override') is explicit. Next Mode 3 run should pick up PROP lifecycle verification.
deferred_to_next_mode_3_run: PROP-030 verification (backlog-trend monitoring — if implemented, validate it's emitting the oldest_open_item_age_days metric used by PROP-031)
